summary: The main claim is: The problem is that major governments and militaries are overwhelmingly incentivised and well-positioned to exploit those capabilities for offensive and defensive information warfare.
Information warfare has long hinged on SOTA psychological knowledge and persuasive skill.
The main claim is: “The problem is that we don’t really know what final goal Friendlyface pursues,” the team explains.
The AI safety team is given four additional weeks to test Friendlyface as they deem appropriate.

The main claim is: I think Eliezer made a mistake, there; superintelligent AI with (light-cone-breaking, as opposed to within-light-cone-of-creation) FTL, if you game it out the whole way, actually mostly solves the Fermi Paradox.

Hence, by the anthropic principle, we should expect to be either the first or extremely close to it (and AIUI, frequency arguments like those in the Grabby Aliens paper suggest that "first in entire universe" should usually be significantl…
The main claim is: Except for the ‘Monday - Tuesday’ task, the correct day is above all other days at the end of layer 9.
This means that we ablate all heads in layer 0, layer 1, …, and layer 8, so the layer MLP is not ablated.

The main claim is: I agree with doomers that the space of possible goals that AGI could have is extremely big with many existentially catastrophic goals, but I think that narrowing it down to a much smaller subspace with a much higher fraction of safe goals…
I actually don’t think we need radical innovations in AI safety research to accomplish this.
The main claim is: More specifically: within my lifetime, I think it more likely than not that it will become possible and financially feasible to create and deploy powerful AI agents.

That is, as far as I can tell, there is a disturbingly high risk (I think: greater than 10%) that I live to see the human species permanently and involuntarily disempowered by AI systems we’ve lost control over.
The main claim is: Finally, there should be some “End Task” text string the AI can output indicating that it thinks it has accomplished the goal.
Such a system might have a world model that only includes the top of the table where it is performing the task and the items and robotic arm.
The main claim is: We might be able to train AI alignment assistants that massively accelerate/improve the alignment research that gets done.

These assistants need not have (strongly) superhuman capabilities or be highly agentic, they just need to be capable and aligned enough to allow us to offload most work on alignment, safety, and related problems to them.
The main claim is: It provides an easy-to-understand overview of the main methodology of Cotra’s report.

The main claim is: Similarly, if we say that social instincts are convergent properties of aligned AGI, I think that we should study them as well, and brain enthusiasts might be especially useful here.
In that case, we should study neural sciences and cognitive science to get a better idea of what such a future AGI might look like.
The main claim is: Abstraction: The ability to abstract over combinations of mental primitives.
It's trivial because the heuristics only need to be adjusted in known ways.

The main claim is: So if you think someone is doing this optimization your best estimate of the true strength of the position they're pushing should be lower than if you think they're optimizing less (given otherwise the same observations).
I don't think the extra penalty for incentive shaping needs to be too big, but I do think it should be nonzero.
